# Phase D Audit — Search Trust, Entity Authority, and Internal Linking

**Date:** 2026-06-13  
**Scope:** Public GitHub Pages site (`dkharlanau.github.io`) after Phase C merges (#191, #193, #194).  
**Baseline branch:** `main`  
**Audit data:** `reports/audit/phase-d-audit-data.json` (generated by `scripts/phase_d_audit.py`).  
**Author:** Dzmitryi Kharlanau  
**Status:** Review candidate — findings are descriptive; no verification status was promoted.

---

## 1. Executive Summary

Phase C added three AI-readable surfaces: an updated `llms.txt` manifest, a verified-page inventory (`ai/verified-pages.json`), and a Jekyll `glossary` collection with `DefinedTerm` schema. Phase D maps how those surfaces fit into the broader entity graph, sitemap coverage, and internal link structure.

The site is technically healthy — all automated validation gates pass with only non-critical page-quality warnings. The main risks are **entity consolidation friction** (one duplicated JSON-LD `@id`), **sparse internal linking** to utility and hub pages, and **missing entity metadata** on collection indexes. None of these are emergencies, but they are the next blockers to stronger search and AI-agent trust signals.

| Metric | Pre-fix | Post-fix (this branch) |
|---|---|---|
| Total built HTML pages | 716 | 716 |
| Indexable pages | 381 | 381 |
| Noindex pages | 335 | 335 |
| Orphan indexable pages (0 inlinks) | 63 | 63 |
| Near-orphan indexable pages (≤2 inlinks) | 130 | 130 |
| Duplicate top-level JSON-LD `@id` across pages | 1 | 0 |
| Verified pages missing `author` | 21 | 0 |
| Verified Atlas section indexes missing `tags` | 9 | 9 |
| Glossary pages with non-zero incoming links | 9 | 9 |
| `llms.txt` machine-endpoint URL count | 17 | 19 |

**Low-risk fixes applied in this branch:**

1. Removed duplicate `DataCatalog` `@id` by emitting the catalog block only on `/ai/`.
2. Added a stable `@id` to the homepage `WebSite` JSON-LD so `isPartOf` references resolve to a single entity.
3. Added `author: Dzmitryi Kharlanau` to all 21 verified Atlas/Skill Hub collection index pages.
4. Added `ai/verified-pages.json`, `ai/atlas-compact-index.json`, and `ai/profile-audit.json` to `sitemap-data.xml`.
5. Updated `_includes/llms-manifest.txt` (v1.11) to list `ai/verified-pages.json` and `/atlas/glossary/` as machine endpoints and in retrieval guidance.
6. Hardened `scripts/phase_d_audit.py` to ignore intentionally reusable global entity IDs (`#website`, `#dkharlanau`, `#organization`) when flagging duplicate `@id` values.

---

## 2. Methodology

`scripts/phase_d_audit.py` reads:

- Built HTML in `_site/` for robots directives, canonical URLs, local `<a>` links, and JSON-LD blocks.
- Source frontmatter for `status`, `verified`, `author`, `tags`, `last_reviewed`, etc.
- `ai/verified-pages.json`, `llms-full.txt`, `_includes/llms-manifest.txt`, and section sitemaps.

It then computes:

- Indexable vs noindex counts.
- Orphan and near-orphan indexable pages based on indexable incoming links.
- Duplicate top-level JSON-LD `@id` values across pages.
- Verified-inventory / sitemap / `llms-full.txt` mismatches.
- Verified pages missing author, date, or tags.
- Glossary pages that are `noindex` but already receive internal links.

---

## 3. Findings

### 3.1 Entity graph: duplicate `DataCatalog` `@id` (fixed)

**Severity:** Medium  
**Pre-fix count:** 1 duplicated `@id` appearing on 18 pages.

The JSON-LD block for `https://dkharlanau.github.io/ai/catalog.json#catalog` was emitted on `/ai/` **and** on every dataset catalog page (`/datasets/`, `/datasets/ams/`, `/datasets/types/`, etc.). Search engines and AI crawlers consolidate entities by `@id`; emitting the same canonical catalog ID on many detail pages creates ambiguity about which page is the authoritative catalog.

**Fix applied:** `_includes/seo/structured-data.html` now emits the `DataCatalog` block only when `page.url == '/ai/'`. Dataset catalog pages retain `CollectionPage` markup and `BreadcrumbList`. `docs/seo/structured-data-google-audit.md` was updated to reflect the new policy.

### 3.2 WebSite entity lacked a stable `@id` (fixed)

**Severity:** Low  
**Pre-fix count:** n/a

The `WebSite` JSON-LD in `_includes/head.html` did not have an `@id`, but every `WebPage` / `Article` JSON-LD block referenced `{{ site_home }}#website` in `isPartOf`. Without a matching `@id`, the reference points to a node that is not explicitly declared.

**Fix applied:** Added `"@id": "https://dkharlanau.github.io/#website"` to the `WebSite` block.

### 3.3 Verified collection indexes missing `author` (fixed)

**Severity:** Low  
**Pre-fix count:** 21 pages

Atlas and Skill Hub collection index pages (`/atlas/*/index.md`, `/skill-hub/*/index.md`) are verified, indexable, and sitemap-included, but had no `author` frontmatter. This left their `Article` / `TechArticle` JSON-LD without an explicit author reference on hub pages.

**Fix applied:** Added `author: "Dzmitryi Kharlanau"` to all 21 index pages. A Jekyll default was also added to `_config.yml` so future Atlas/Skill Hub pages inherit the author unless overridden.

### 3.4 Verified Atlas section indexes missing `tags`

**Severity:** Low  
**Count:** 9 pages

The nine Atlas section indexes (`/atlas/ai-operations/` … `/atlas/sap/`) do not have `tags` frontmatter. Tags are optional for index pages, but they feed `keywords`/`mentions` in JSON-LD and help cluster classification in maintenance scans. Because tags are section-specific, this requires a manual pass rather than a safe default.

**Recommendation:** Add 3–5 section-relevant tags to each Atlas index page (e.g., `sap-ams`, `master-data`, `integration`). Do not add generic filler tags.

### 3.5 Orphan indexable pages

**Severity:** Medium (for discovery)  
**Count:** 63 pages with zero indexable incoming links.

Examples include:

- `.well-known/agent-skills/*` utility pages
- `/agent-skills/`, `/certifications/`, `/changelog/`
- Docs pages (`/docs/ai/*`, `/docs/atlas/*`, `/docs/seo/*`, etc.)
- `/legal/*`, `/news/*`, `/notes/`, `/publications/`
- Several `/reports/seo/*` pages
- `/radar/*` dated signal pages

Some of these are intentionally standalone (docs, legal, news, radar) and are reachable only from sitemaps or external references. Others are utility surfaces that would benefit from at least one header/footer or hub link:

- `/certifications/` — link from `/about/` or footer.
- `/changelog/` — link from footer or `/news/`.
- `/agent-skills/` — link from `/skill-hub/` or `/ai/`.
- `/docs/ai/CONTENT_VERIFICATION_POLICY/` and related docs — link from `/about/`, `/ai/`, or footer.

**Recommendation:** Add a small, restrained set of footer/header links for utility surfaces. Keep the minimal visual design; do not turn the header into a full mega-menu. Coordinate with existing issue #141 (global navigation).

### 3.6 Near-orphan verified pages

**Severity:** Medium  
**Count:** 130 indexable pages with only 1–2 indexable incoming links.

Many verified Atlas and Skill Hub articles are reachable only from their parent section index. This limits PageRank/authority flow and makes them hard for crawlers to discover if the parent index is not well-linked.

**Recommendation:** Strengthen cross-linking between related verified pages using:

- “Related pages” sections at the bottom of high-traffic verified articles.
- Topic-cluster navigation on section index pages.
- Cross-links between Atlas diagnostics and Skill Hub operating skills.

Coordinate with existing issues #140 (scenario-to-Atlas linking) and #145 (Skill Hub to agent-skills linking).

### 3.7 Glossary pages receive links but default to `noindex`

**Severity:** Low / strategic  
**Count:** 9 glossary pages with non-zero incoming links.

The glossary collection defaults to `robots: noindex, follow`, `sitemap: false`, `status: needs_verification`. Internal links already point to glossary terms, which is valid because `follow` is allowed. The pages have good `DefinedTerm` / `DefinedTermSet` schema. The question is whether to keep them noindex working material or promote selected terms to indexable after review.

**Recommendation:** Decide on a glossary indexing policy before scaling the collection. Options:

1. Keep all glossary pages noindex until each term is individually reviewed.
2. Promote a small starter set of high-value terms (e.g., `order-to-cash`, `business-partner`, `clean-core`) to indexable with `reviewed`/`verified:true`.

Do not mass-promote without human review.

### 3.8 `llms.txt` did not reference new Phase C surfaces (fixed)

**Severity:** Low  
**Pre-fix count:** `llms.txt` listed 17 machine endpoints; `ai/verified-pages.json` and `/atlas/glossary/` were absent.

AI agents consuming `llms.txt` had no direct pointer to the verified inventory or the glossary, reducing the discoverability of Phase C work.

**Fix applied:** `_includes/llms-manifest.txt` bumped to v1.11 and now lists:

- Verified page inventory (JSON): `https://dkharlanau.github.io/ai/verified-pages.json`
- Atlas glossary definitions: `https://dkharlanau.github.io/atlas/glossary/`

Retrieval guidance was updated to mention both endpoints.

### 3.9 `sitemap-data.xml` omitted new machine endpoints (fixed)

**Severity:** Low  
**Pre-fix count:** 10 data URLs.

`sitemap-data.xml` did not include `ai/verified-pages.json`, `ai/atlas-compact-index.json`, or `ai/profile-audit.json`, even though these are canonical machine-readable surfaces referenced from the Person entity and `llms.txt`.

**Fix applied:** Added the three JSON endpoints to `sitemap-data.xml`.

### 3.10 One verified Atlas page is not linked from top hubs

**Severity:** Low  
**Count:** 1

`/atlas/` itself is the only verified Atlas page not linked from any higher-level hub page. This is a minor navigation gap because `/atlas/` is linked from the homepage, footer, and `llms.txt`, but not from another hub/index page.

**Recommendation:** Ensure `/atlas/` is linked from `/ai/` and/or the footer so hub-to-hub navigation is complete.

---

## 4. Validation Results

All automated gates pass after the fixes in this branch:

```text
252 passed in 17.56s
Public repo hygiene check passed for 1151 tracked files.
SEO checks passed for 716 HTML files.
Structured data check passed for 716 HTML files.
Indexing policy check passed.
Atlas artifact check PASSED — all artifacts are up-to-date and valid.
No broken local links detected.
```

`check_page_quality.py` reports only non-critical warnings on Skill Hub index pages where H1/title/description alignment can be tightened. These are pre-existing and are tracked separately (see existing issues #141, #144, #153).

## 6. Files Changed

Content / configuration:

- `_config.yml` — added default `author` for `atlas` and `skill-hub` paths.
- `_includes/head.html` — added `@id` to `WebSite` JSON-LD.
- `_includes/seo/structured-data.html` — restricted `DataCatalog` block to `/ai/`.
- `_includes/llms-manifest.txt` — v1.11; added verified inventory and glossary references.
- `sitemap-data.xml` — added `ai/verified-pages.json`, `ai/atlas-compact-index.json`, `ai/profile-audit.json`.
- `docs/seo/structured-data-google-audit.md` — updated dataset/AI catalog schema policy.
- `atlas/index.md`, `atlas/*/index.md` (8 files) — added `author`.
- `skill-hub/index.md`, `skill-hub/*/index.md` (11 files) — added `author`.

Generated artifacts:

- `ai/verified-pages.json`, `ai/atlas-compact-index.json`, `llms-full.txt`, `atlas/manifest.json`, and related files regenerated by `scripts/generate_atlas_artifacts.py`.

Audit tooling:

- `scripts/phase_d_audit.py` — new; collects Phase D metrics and filters reusable entity IDs from duplicate-`@id` reporting.
- `reports/audit/phase-d-audit-data.json` — machine-readable audit output.
- `docs/audits/phase-d-search-trust-entity-authority-audit-2026-06-13.md` — this report.

---

## 7. Next Steps

1. **Human review** the audit report and the low-risk fixes in this branch.
2. **Merge** the fixes if approved; no verification status was changed.
3. **Manual content pass** for Atlas index `tags` (issue #4 above).
4. **Navigation design decision** for orphan utility pages (issue #5; coordinate with #141).
5. **Cross-linking sprint** for near-orphan verified pages (issue #6).
6. **Glossary indexing policy** decision (issue #7).
7. **CI hardening** for duplicate `@id` detection (issue #10).

---

## 8. Safety Notes

- No private corpus, client data, or internal incident details were exposed.
- No page was promoted to `verified: true` or `status: reviewed` without human review.
- Strategic content decisions (tagging, navigation, glossary promotion, cross-linking strategy) are left as tracked issues, not auto-implemented.
